typecheck.observation: No typecheck configured for this JavaScript project

lint.observation: 0 errors, 4 warnings (all pre-existing no-unused-vars in test files)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When using browser globals (URL, Buffer, etc.) in Electron main process files, add the appropriate /* global */ ESLint directive at the top of the file to satisfy no-undef. Example: /* global URL */ for main.js.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ipc-input-validation added /* global URL */ at main.js:1 to satisfy ESLint's no-undef rule for the URL constructor, which is available in Electron's main process via V8 but not in ESLint's default node environment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the IPC Input Validation Pattern example to show the actual pattern used in production: validate typeof first, then parse URL inside the existing try/catch alongside the operation. This matches the real implementation pattern and is more concise. The current example shows a separate try/catch for URL parsing which works but differs from the actual implementation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ipc-input-validation implemented URL validation differently from the skill example. The skill shows 'try { const parsed = new URL(param); ... } catch { return error }' as a standalone block, but the actual implementation wraps validation + shell.openExternal in one try/catch where malformed URLs are caught by the generic error handler.

rejectedObservations.observation: architecture.md already documents IPC validation patterns (path traversal prevention, URL protocol whitelist, hostname-based navigation)
